spec_match.rationale: Touches multi-select, base-select, CSS from the chromestatus summary but skips single-select — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Some scripted state (select) but no user-driven control flow — borderline static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6222145025867776 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on popup-vs-listbox
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Customizable select listbox introduces single-select but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11758

openQuestions.title: Add a user-driven control to popup-vs-listbox
openQuestions.detail: Scripted page state exists but the user can't drive the change. Add at least one button / range / select that invokes the API in-page, and re-render the result so the reader can see input → output.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on popup-vs-listbox
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: popup-vs-listbox covers the happy path of Customizable select listbox and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
